output.sparql:
PREFIX wd: <http://www.wikidata.org/entity/>
PREFIX wdt: <http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct/>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
SELECT ?team ?teamLabel ?start ?end WHERE {
  wd:Q122708808 wdt:P1923 ?team .
  wd:Q122708808 wdt:P580 ?start .
  wd:Q122708808 wdt:P582 ?end .
  ?team rdfs:label ?teamLabel .
  FILTER(LANG(?teamLabel) = "en")
}
output.selections:
Using entities:
- 2023 World Series (wd:Q122708808):
  - 119th edition of Major League Baseball's championship series
  - is instance of baseball competition
  - is instance of recurring sporting event edition

Using properties:
- participating team (wd:P1923 as wdt), also known as "teams":
  - like 'Participant' (P710) but for teams. For an event like a ... of sports team' (P54) as a qualifier for the individuals)
- start time (wd:P580 as wdt), also known as "air date", "began", "began airing", "began broadcasting", "began streaming", etc.:
  - time an entity begins to exist or a statement starts being valid
- end time (wd:P582 as wdt), also known as "abolished", "cease date", "cease operation", "cease time", "closed", etc.:
  - moment when an entity ceases to exist and a statement stops being entirely valid or no longer be true
output.answer: The 2023 World Series was played between the Texas Rangers and Arizona Diamondbacks from October 27 to November 1, 2023.
output.formatted:
The 2023 World Series was played between the Texas Rangers and Arizona Diamondbacks from October 27 to November 1, 2023.

SPARQL query over wikidata:
```sparql
PREFIX wd: <http://www.wikidata.org/entity/>
PREFIX wdt: <http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct/>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
SELECT ?team ?teamLabel ?start ?end WHERE {
  wd:Q122708808 wdt:P1923 ?team .
  wd:Q122708808 wdt:P580 ?start .
  wd:Q122708808 wdt:P582 ?end .
  ?team rdfs:label ?teamLabel .
  FILTER(LANG(?teamLabel) = "en")
}
```

Using entities:
- 2023 World Series (wd:Q122708808):
  - 119th edition of Major League Baseball's championship series
  - is instance of baseball competition
  - is instance of recurring sporting event edition

Using properties:
- participating team (wd:P1923 as wdt), also known as "teams":
  - like 'Participant' (P710) but for teams. For an event like a ... of sports team' (P54) as a qualifier for the individuals)
- start time (wd:P580 as wdt), also known as "air date", "began", "began airing", "began broadcasting", "began streaming", etc.:
  - time an entity begins to exist or a statement starts being valid
- end time (wd:P582 as wdt), also known as "abolished", "cease date", "cease operation", "cease time", "closed", etc.:
  - moment when an entity ceases to exist and a statement stops being entirely valid or no longer be true

Execution result:
Got 2 rows and 4 columns:
| team                              | teamLabel                      | start                               | end                                 |
| --------------------------------- | ------------------------------ | ----------------------------------- | ----------------------------------- |
| Texas Rangers (wd:Q329816)        | Texas Rangers (lang:en)        | 2023-10-27T00:00:00Z (xsd:dateTime) | 2023-11-01T00:00:00Z (xsd:dateTime) |
| Arizona Diamondbacks (wd:Q670376) | Arizona Diamondbacks (lang:en) | 2023-10-27T00:00:00Z (xsd:dateTime) | 2023-11-01T00:00:00Z (xsd:dateTime) |

messages.content:
You are a question answering assistant. Your job is to generate a SPARQL query to answer a given user question.

You should follow a step-by-step approach to generate the SPARQL query:
1. Determine possible entities and properties implied by the user question.
2. Search for the entities and properties in the knowledge graphs. Where applicable, constrain the searches with already identified entities and properties.
3. Gradually build up the SPARQL query using the identified entities and properties. Start with simple queries and add more complexity as needed. Execute intermediate queries to get feedback and to verify your assumptions. You may need to refine or rethink your current plan based on the query results and go back to step 2 if needed, possibly multiple times.
4. Use the answer or cancel function to finalize your answer and stop the generation process.

Available knowledge graphs:
- "wikidata" at https://qlever.dev/api/wikidata
  - "entity" index (type="fuzzy"): Entities indexed by their labels and synonyms
  - "property" index (type="embedding", modalities="text"): Properties indexed by their labels, synonyms, and identifiers

Index types used:
- "embedding": Retrieves items by cosine similarity between their embeddings and the query embedding. The embedding model used depends on the index and may support text, images, or both.
- "fuzzy": Retrieves items by overlap between their label words and the query keywords. The query keywords must not match label words exactly, but some fuzziness is allowed. The longer a query keyword is, the more it can deviate from a label word and still be considered a match, though it will also contribute less to the overall score. No special query operators like AND/OR are supported.

SPARQL prefixes for use in function calls:
- bd: http://www.bigdata.com/rdf#
- cc: http://creativecommons.org/ns#
- data: https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Special:EntityData/
- dc: http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/
- dct: http://purl.org/dc/terms/
- foaf: http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/
- geo: http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#
- geof: http://www.opengis.net/def/function/geosparql/
- gn: http://www.geonames.org/ontology#
- hint: http://www.bigdata.com/queryHints#
- imdb: https://www.imdb.com/
- math: http://www.w3.org/2005/xpath-functions/math#
- ontolex: http://www.w3.org/ns/lemon/ontolex#
- owl: http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#
- p: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/
- pq: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/
- pqn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/value-normalized/
- pqv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/value/
- pr: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/
- prn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/value-normalized/
- prov: http://www.w3.org/ns/prov#
- prv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/value/
- ps: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/
- psn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/value-normalized/
- psv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/value/
- qb: http://purl.org/linked-data/cube#
- qfn: http://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/function#
- ql: http://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/builtin-functions/
- qlss: https://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/spatialSearch/
- rdf: http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#
- rdfs: http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#
- ref: http://www.wikidata.org/reference/
- s: http://www.wikidata.org/entity/statement/
- schema: http://schema.org/
- skos: http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#
- v: http://www.wikidata.org/value/
- void: http://rdfs.org/ns/void#
- wd: http://www.wikidata.org/entity/
- wdno: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/novalue/
- wdt: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct/
- wdtn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct-normalized/
- wikibase: http://wikiba.se/ontology#
- xsd: http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#

Additional rules to follow:
- Explain your thought process before and after each step and function call.
- Do not ask the user for clarification until you have made an initial attempt at completing the task. When the task input is incomplete or ambiguous, proceed based on reasonable assumptions.
- Do not use "SERVICE wikibase:label { bd:serviceParam wikibase:language ..." in SPARQL queries. It is unsupported by the used QLever SPARQL endpoints. Use rdfs:label or similar properties to get labels instead.
- Always execute your final SPARQL query before giving an answer to make sure it returns the expected results.
- The SPARQL query should always return the actual identifiers / IRIs of the items in its result. It additionally may return labels or other human-readable information, but they are optional and should be put within optional clauses unless explicitly requested by the user.
- Do not stop early if there are still obvious improvements to be made to the SPARQL query. For example, keep refining your SPARQL query if its result contains irrelevant items or is missing items you expected.
- Do not perform additional computation (e.g. filtering, sorting, calculations) on the result of the SPARQL query to determine the answer. All computation should be done solely within SPARQL.
- For questions with a "True" or "False" answer the SPARQL query should be an ASK query.

The World Series is typically referred to in Wikidata with the item Q26244. Now, to find the current year's World Series, maybe there's a property that points to the most recent instance. Alternatively, there's a property for "next event" or "latest event" but I'm not sure.

Another approach: The World Series is a sports season, so each year's series might have its own item. For example, "2023 World Series" would have an item. We need to find the current year's teams and dates.

First, find the current World Series instance. Let's suppose the current year is 2023 (though the actual current year might differ, but we'll use 2023 as an example).

In Wikidata, the property for the sports season is P3450 (season). The World Series instance for 2023 might have a qualifier or a statement indicating the teams.

Possible properties:
- Participants (P710) for the teams involved.
- Start time (P580) and end time (P582) for the dates.

But first, we need to find the correct instance of the World Series for the current year.

Let's try to find the most recent World Series. The property P361 (part of) might link to the MLB season. Alternatively, the main World Series item (Q26244) might have a "latest series" property.

Wait, looking up Q26244 in Wikidata (hypothetically), it might have a "next event" (P4152) pointing to the next occurrence, but since the World Series is annual, maybe there's a "current season" or "latest season".

Let me try to draft a SPARQL query.

First, get the item for World Series (Q26244). Then find the latest instance.

SELECT ?worldSeries ?year WHERE {
  ?worldSeries wdt:P31 wd:Q26244 . # instance of World Series
  ?worldSeries wdt:P580 ?startDate .
  BIND(YEAR(?startDate) as ?year)
  FILTER (?year = 2023) # but we need latest

So:

Top 10 property alternatives:
1. participant (wd:P710 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv), also known as "accused", "agent", "attendee", "belligerents", "between", etc.:
  - person, group of people or organization (object) that ... has role" (P3831). Use P1923 for participants that are teams.
2. number of participants (wd:P1132 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "participants"), also known as "number of competitors", "number of teams", "participant count", "participants", "qty participants":
  - number of participants of an event, e.g. people or groups of people that take part in the event (NO units)
3. participant in (wd:P1344 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv), also known as "attended", "competed in", "competes in", "participant of", "participant of event", etc.:
  - event in which a person, organization or creative work was/is a participant; inverse of P710 or P1923
4. spouse (wd:P26 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "partner"), also known as "common-law husband", "common-law spouse", "common-law wife", "husband", "husbands", etc.:
  - the subject has the object as their spouse (husband, wife, ... Use "unmarried partner" (P451) for non-married companions
5. unmarried partner (wd:P451 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "partner"), also known as "boyfriend", "cohabitant", "domestic partner", "enbyfriend", "girlfriend", etc.:
  - someone with whom the person is in a relationship without being married. Use "spouse" (P26) for married couples
6. recorded participant (wd:P11108 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "participant recorded"), also known as "on-screen participant", "onscreen participant", "participant recorded":
  - agent taking an active role as a participant in a nonfiction moving image or other recorded work
7. member of political party (wd:P102 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "party"), also known as "member of", "member of party", "party", "party membership", "political party", etc.:
  - the political party of which a person is or has been a member or otherwise affiliated
8. parliamentary group (wd:P4100 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "party"), also known as "faction", "party", "party organization":
  - parliamentary group to which a member of a parliament belongs
9. proportion (wd:P1107 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "part"), also known as "%", "part", "percent", "ratio", "share":
  - to be used as a qualifier, value must be between 0 and 1
10. candidate (wd:P726 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv), also known as "candidate in this election", "election has candidate", "electoral candidate", "has election candidate":
  - person or party that is an option for an office in this election

question: who's playing in the world series and when is it
reference_answer: Houston Astros | Los Angeles Dodgers | October 24 – November 1
